MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 100043581) FILED BY DEFENDANT J.J. ZHU CORPORATION ERRONEOUSLY SUED AND SERVED HEREIN AS J.J. ZHU CORP. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION
MINI MINUTES FOR AUG-24-2018 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE AUG-24-2018 HEARING ON DEFENDANT J.J. ZHU CORPORATION'S DEMURRER, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. NOTWITHSTANDING THE WORKERS' COMPENSATION REGIME, EMPLOYERS MAY BE SUED FOR ASSAULTS BY THEIR EMPLOYEES UNDER AUTHORIZATION AND/OR RATIFICATION THEORIES; THE COMPLAINT ADEQUATELY SO PLEADS. (SEE, E.G., IVERSON V. ATLAS PACIFIC ENGINEERING (1983) 143 CAL.APP.3D 219, 228-29; HERRICK V. QUALITY HOTELS, INNS & RESORTS, INC. (1993) 19 CAL.APP.4TH 1608 1618.) FURTHER, DEFENDANT RAISES DISPUTES OF FACT NOT APPROPRIATE FOR A DEMURRER. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 AND WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, EMAIL: TORREANOM@AOL.COM. (302/RBU)

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE AUG-24-2018 HEARING ON DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE, THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING. THE MOTION IS DENIED FOR THE SAME REASONS ITS JOINTLY FILED DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. ALSO, THE COMPLAINT ADEQUATELY PLEADS GROUNDS FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 AND WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, EMAIL: TORREANOM@AOL.COM. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-24-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Robert S. Nelson, Esq. of Nelson Law Group, 415.702.9869, appearing for the Plaintiff;
Alexandra Martino, Esq. of Dunbar & Associates, 562.491.7600, appearing by CourtCall for the Defendant.

Ruling: ARGUED. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on the NOTICE OF HEARING ON DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT as follows: Defendant J.J. Zhu Corporation's demurrer is OVERRULED. Notwithstanding the workers' compensation regime, employers may be sued for assaults by their employees under authorization and/or ratification theories; the complaint adequately so pleads. (See, e.g., Iverson v. Atlas Pacific Engineering (1983) 143 Cal.App.3d 219, 228-29; Herrick v. Quality Hotels, Inns & Resorts, Inc. (1993) 19 Cal.App.4th 1608 1618.) Further, defendant raises disputes of fact not appropriate for a demurrer. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/RBU)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-24-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Robert S. Nelson, Esq. of Nelson Law Group, 415.702.9869, appearing for the Plaintiff;
Alexandra Martino, Esq. of Dunbar & Associates, 562.491.7600, appearing by CourtCall for the Defendant.

Ruling: ARGUED. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on the NOTICE OF HEARING ON DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED OMPLAINT as follows: Defendant's motion to strike is DENIED for the same reasons its jointly filed demurrer is overruled. Also, the complaint adequately pleads grounds for punitive damages. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/RBU)
